answer: Rate limiting in BUGS has three design flaws (BUGS-85 root, plus subtasks BUGS-86/87/88): (1) CORS preflight OPTIONS requests are counted against the user's quota, doubling consumption for any non-simple browser request - one customer integration sends 50 actual requests but consumes 100 quota units (i.e., users hit limits at half their actual volume); (2) X-RateLimit-* headers are not set on OPTIONS responses, so client-side adaptive logic can't see remaining quota; (3) limits keyed by IP rather than API key for authenticated traffic, missing per-key/per-OAuth-scope granularity. Fix path: early-return for OPTIONS in middleware (BUGS-86), comprehensive rate-limit headers including X-RateLimit-Resource (BUGS-87), per-key configurable limits, 429 with Retry-After, and a user-facing rate-limit dashboard (BUGS-88). Related: BUGS-22 covers a separate distributed-credential-stuffing detection problem because per-IP limits are bypassed by residential proxies.

(3) No OCR for extracting error message text from screenshots. (4) No auto-suggestion of troubleshooting guides when a ticket contains text or images matching a known error pattern. (5) No one-click apply with customization for the specific user, no measurement of guide effectiveness (was the issue resolved after sending?), no admin interface for engineering to add new error patterns. (6) Localization gap - error messages vary by language and need handling. Subtasks: CRM-88 builds the agent-side suggestion UI (non-intrusive panel with matched error, confidence score, one-click apply that populates a response template, and acceptance-rate tracking). Root cause: KB content exists but isn't programmatically addressable from agent workflows.

answer: Medium-priority CRM issues (84 tickets) target incident-management gaps in four areas: (1) Ticket-to-incident linking and duplicate prevention - CRM-96 shows agents a banner when an active incident matches ticket keywords with one-click linking, and CRM-92 integrates form data into ticket display so agents can filter (e.g., all iOS 16 issues). Mobile rendering/perf bottlenecks per BUGS-72: (1) Markdown/rich-text rendering happens on the main thread during scroll - inline images and code blocks in issue descriptions trigger expensive layout work synchronously. (2) Avatar images are loaded synchronously, blocking scroll. (3) Cell-height calculation is expensive and not cached, recomputed every layout pass (subtask BUGS-75 'Optimize cell height calculation with estimation and caching'). (4) Too many reused cells - 100+ item lists hold 50 cells in memory simultaneously. Result: frame rate drops to 15-20fps on mid-range devices, scrolling 'feels like an Android phone from 2015' per user feedback, hurting App Store ratings. Fix path: pre-render markdown off main thread, skeleton loading for images during scroll, cell-height caching after first layout, virtual list rendering so only visible cells are instantiated, and no scroll-related ANRs on Android. Goal: maintain 60fps scrolling on devices 3 years old or newer. Adjacent rendering bottleneck - BUGS-52 iOS crashes loading 15MB+ attachments fully into memory instead of using QuickLook (BUGS-54) for streaming/progressive loads.

args.jql: project = BUGS AND (description ~ render OR description ~ frame OR summary ~ scroll)
